title: Commodities Brief — 14 May 2026

sections.heading: Overview
sections.content:
Commodities Markets — Session Overview

Commodities remain range-bound within the broader supercycle framework as dollar strength weighs on precious metals while agricultural markets surge on weather concerns. Risk sentiment stays cautiously optimistic despite ongoing geopolitical headwinds.

Precious Metals
Gold trades sideways at $4705.80/oz, consolidating recent gains as real rates stabilize and central bank demand provides underlying support. Silver underperforms at $87.54/oz (-1.52% intraday) on industrial demand concerns. Platinum weakens to $2142.10/oz amid automotive sect

sections.heading: Analysis
sections.content:
or headwinds, while palladium holds $1516.00/oz. Copper retreats to $6.58/lb, signaling near-term growth uncertainty despite constructive medium-term fundamentals.

Energy
Oil markets maintain strength with WTI at $101.09/bbl and Brent at $105.79/bbl, supported by tight global inventories and robust summer driving season expectations. The $4.70/bbl Brent-WTI spread reflects persistent midstream bottlenecks. Natural gas finds footing at $2.88/MMBtu on cooling demand forecasts.

Agriculture
Grain complex rallies sharply on adverse weather patterns across key growing regions. Wheat

sections.heading: Outlook
sections.content:
surges to $678.25/bu (+9.00% weekly) on European drought concerns and Black Sea export disruptions. Corn climbs to $478.50/bu on planting delays, while soybeans hold steady at $1217.25/bu.

Trade Ideas
LONG: Wheat (weather premium expanding), WTI crude (inventory drawdowns), Gold (CB buying intact)
SHORT/HEDGE: Silver (industrial demand lag), Natural gas (oversupplied market)

Key Risks
Dollar breakout above 108 DXY could pressure metals complex. Sudden weather normalization threatens grain rally sustainability. OPEC+ production discipline remains uncertain ahead of June meeting.
